baseline_protocol.source: Hunter Williams + Jay Campbell Cheat Sheet 2023

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider Hunter's conservative longevity floor as the operative starting frame for the no-DAC variant: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "CJC-1295 (no DAC) 100–200 mcg + Ipamorelin 100–200 mcg" and "Taken subcutaneous, at night, on an empty stomach." The no-DAC form is what Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:08:32.039): "I would do CJC no DAC" and Taylor (xIjeNpzC2Lc 00:04:46.240): "if I'm going to be using CJC 1259 by itself I'm going to be using it without the DAC" both prefer; we'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh that against Trevor (l1QK_Vmlhgk 00:10:27.360): "CJC 1295 with DAC once a week produced significantly more consistent and biologically favorable and active IGF elevation." Cycle, don't run forever: Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:37:08.000): "If you run this entire stack 24/7 365, you will 100% demolish your pituitary in about 16 weeks."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- **Experts do not explicitly differentiate "no DAC" from the parent CJC-1295 card in this corpus** — every dose, route, and frequency cited below is identical to what the experts teach for CJC-1295 generally; the no-DAC distinction surfaces only as preference statements (Hunter/Taylor prefer no-DAC for shorter half-life and more pulsatile release; Trevor prefers DAC for sustained IGF elevation). Treat this card as a pointer to the parent CJC-1295 card with the no-DAC preference flagged.
- Pair only with a GHRP, never another GHRH: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "You don’t combine multiple GHRH analogs (like CJC + Tesamorelin + Sermorelin). That’s just three versions of the same signal" and "what you do want is one GHRH analog + one GHRP."
- Flushing/heart rate at higher doses: Hunter email threadId 199d026a6dd3eb61 (2025-10-10): "CJC-1295 no DAC at 300 mcg gave me super flushing and a higher heart rate. Should I drop to 200 mcg for a bit?" Answer: "Yes. That warm, flushed “rush” is common when you jump in hot with CJC. Titrate down to 150–200 mcg until your body adapts." Taylor (Of9flZsatFY 00:10:43.000): "a lot of the flushing kind of comes from the CJC not So Much from the epin."
- Water retention with CJC: Taylor (xIjeNpzC2Lc 00:10:53.480): "it can retain water so that’s another reason why I like take it at night."
- DAC vs no-DAC dispute (the core reason this card exists separately): Trevor (l1QK_Vmlhgk 00:09:27.279): "CJC with DAC, and this isn’t a matter of debate” the peptide circulates for over a week"; Taylor (U3Yqjb3bK6A 00:04:50.000): "I personally am never going to recommend using CJC with Dak because that is something that you’re going to have harsher side effects with CJC with no deck you don’t get the harsh side effects."
- Pituitary desensitization if uncycled: Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:37:11.200): "you will 100% demolish your pituitary in about 16 weeks. Your GHR and ghrein receptors will undergo something called clatherine mediated endoccytosis."
- Cancer caveat (active malignancy): Trevor (m_GpIdkHI4M 00:28:28.360): "if you have cancer, no, you shouldn’t take it. If you don’t, you are restoring a vital health parameter." Trevor (m_GpIdkHI4M 00:26:51.080): "It can help an existing cancer? Yeah, grow faster. But it does not cause cancer."
- Carbs/insulin blunt response: Trevor (WIHm62bBsUI 00:23:53.200): "Taking it with food, especially carbs, blunts the release" and (00:24:02.080): "elevated blood glucose and insulin suppress growth hormone secretion."
- Always isolate first: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "Always use each compound in isolation first. That’s how you learn your own response."
- Hunter 2023 cheat-sheet baseline (no-DAC specifically): 10 mg vial reconstituted in 3 mL BAC water, 200 mcg dose = 6 units on a 1 mL insulin syringe, PM, 5 days on / 2 days off, 8 weeks on / 8 weeks off — treat as floor; newer transcript content above narrows the conservative dose to 100–200 mcg.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: When the no-DAC form is the chosen variant, all three experts agree on the pairing and timing. Pair with a GHRP (ipamorelin) for synergy, not another GHRH. Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "For anti-aging and longevity, the best combo is almost always CJC no DAC + Ipamorelin or Tesamorelin + Ipamorelin" and "GHRH analogs are the ‘primers.’ … GHRPs, on the other hand, are the ‘ignition.’" Taylor (xIjeNpzC2Lc 00:11:46.839): "the growth hormone CJC is going to help with the production but you want the" both pathways. Trevor (l1QK_Vmlhgk 00:14:14.959): "CJC and ipamearellin work on different receptor systems within the same organ" and (l1QK_Vmlhgk 00:14:21.110): "CJC 1295 hits GHRH receptors. Ipamarellin hits GHSR1A receptors." Time it at night, empty stomach: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "Taken subcutaneous, at night, on an empty stomach." Taylor (Of9flZsatFY 00:12:25.519): "I always took this right before bed this was like the last thing I did like right before I got into my bed." Trevor (WIHm62bBsUI 00:23:27.280): "the correct protocol administer the secret go combo like CJC and Pamelan hypothetically once a day at night 30 to 60 minutes before you go to bed on an empty stomach." Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:08:36.680): "the GH release, it’s stronger and more sustained with ipamorelin and CJC. There’s minimal to no hunger."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: DAC vs no-DAC is the central dispute and the reason a separate no-DAC card exists. Trevor (l1QK_Vmlhgk 00:10:11.760): "once provides one injection for your kangaroo pituitary stimulation for over a week up to two weeks. So the DAC is a gamecher" and (l1QK_Vmlhgk 00:10:27.360): "CJC 1295 with DAC once a week produced significantly more consistent and biologically favorable and active IGF elevation." Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:08:32.039): "I would do CJC no DAC" and email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11) lists "CJC-1295 (no DAC)" as his canonical pick because it is "short-acting enough to mimic natural GH pulses." Taylor (U3Yqjb3bK6A 00:04:50.000): "I personally am never going to recommend using CJC with Dak because that is something that you’re going to have harsher side effects with CJC with no deck you don’t get the harsh side effects" and Taylor (xIjeNpzC2Lc 00:04:46.240): "if I’m going to be using CJC 1259 by itself I’m going to be using it without the DAC." Cycle length differs even within the no-DAC pick: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "I like running 8–12 week blocks, sometimes switching pathways afterward" and "Run CJC + Ipamorelin for 12 weeks. Then move to Tesamorelin solo for another cycle." Taylor (U3Yqjb3bK6A 00:11:27.079): "with illin tesin CJC we can stay on those peptides for 8 weeks." Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:39:53.119): "All three together, once per night, five days per week, two days off" and (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:37:44.960): "normally I say 7 days a week. I need you to understand this… Once per night." Pairing partner differs: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:59.200): if you want a single best GH peptide, "I would use tesamorelin and or a GLP-1 agonist" — not CJC. Taylor (U3Yqjb3bK6A 00:16:57.399): "more bang for your buck go with the I and CJC or t morelan." Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:39:32.640): runs CJC no-DAC + ipamorelin alongside tesamorelin in a triple-stack — "to Samrian 1 milligram. I am at night… CJC 1295 no DAC 100 micrograms. I am every night within minutes of the tesamarillan. I would do a Pamarillan 200 micrograms. I am every night."

chronic.dose: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "CJC-1295 (no DAC) 100–200 mcg + Ipamorelin 100–200 mcg." Hunter email threadId 199d026a6dd3eb61 (2025-10-10): "Titrate down to 150–200 mcg until your body adapts." Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:39:39.920): "CJC 1295 no DAC 100 micrograms. I am every night." Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:45:13.440): "CJC1 1295, no DAC, 100 micrograms every night. I am." Hunter cheat-sheet 2023 floor: 200 mcg = 6 units on a 1 mL insulin syringe (10 mg vial in 3 mL BAC).
chronic.route: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "Taken subcutaneous, at night, on an empty stomach." Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:39:39.920): "100 micrograms. I am every night within minutes of the tesamarillan" ("I am" = intramuscular). Taylor (Of9flZsatFY 00:11:39.240): "this is a peptide that you do have to inject."
chronic.frequency: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "at night, on an empty stomach." Hunter cheat-sheet 2023: "5 days on, 2 days off" PM. Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:39:53.119): "All three together, once per night, five days per week, two days off." Trevor (WIHm62bBsUI 00:23:27.280): "CJC and Pamelan hypothetically once a day at night 30 to 60 minutes before you go to bed on an empty stomach."
chronic.duration: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "I like running 8–12 week blocks, sometimes switching pathways afterward" and "Run CJC + Ipamorelin for 12 weeks. Then move to Tesamorelin solo for another cycle. Then rest, test IGF-1, and see how your body feels before repeating." Hunter cheat-sheet 2023: "8 weeks on, 8 weeks off." Taylor (U3Yqjb3bK6A 00:11:27.079): "with illin tesin CJC we can stay on those peptides for 8 weeks." Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:37:08.000): "If you run this entire stack 24/7 365, you will 100% demolish your pituitary in about 16 weeks."
chronic.verbatim_citation: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "The conservative longevity stack: CJC-1295 (no DAC) 100–200 mcg + Ipamorelin 100–200 mcg. Taken subcutaneous, at night, on an empty stomach." Trevor (8bL5e8pQc_U 00:39:39.920): "CJC 1295 no DAC 100 micrograms. I am every night within minutes of the tesamarillan."
chronic.timestamp: Hunter email 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11); Trevor 8bL5e8pQc_U 00:39:39.920 and 00:45:13.440; Trevor WIHm62bBsUI 00:23:27.280; Hunter cheat-sheet 2023

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: No expert content in this corpus distinguishes the no-DAC form from the parent CJC-1295 card on female-specific use — all of Taylor's signal applies to both. Carrying through from the parent: Taylor (xIjeNpzC2Lc 00:09:48.760): for ipamorelin she does "morning and at night" "way I have the maximum growth hormone" but for CJC "you want to make sure you’re taking your CJC at night and not in the morning." Taylor (xIjeNpzC2Lc 00:10:53.480): "the other thing with CJC… it can retain water so that’s another reason why I like take it at night." Taylor (Of9flZsatFY 00:01:31.000): "reason why I want wanted to take ellin and CJC was I was going through birth control detox." Taylor (Of9flZsatFY 00:05:09.759): "same thing applies for when you’re um premenopause per menopause like any form of hormonal change." Taylor (Of9flZsatFY 00:05:20.720): "to use if you’re um getting done with breastfeeding." Taylor (Of9flZsatFY 00:07:20.800): "my skin was glowing after taking this um I a lot of women… going through hormonal change." Taylor (U3Yqjb3bK6A 00:09:32.360): "typically my women that are 55 and older they actually don’t get the side effects… of the test marelin water retention as much as my younger women do." Taylor (U3Yqjb3bK6A 00:11:27.079) for ipamorelin/tesamorelin/CJC class: "with illin tesin CJC we can stay on those peptides for 8 weeks." Taylor (U3Yqjb3bK6A 00:04:50.000): "I personally am never going to recommend using CJC with Dak."
